data.title: Stock Market Futures Dip as Oil Prices Climb Again after Iran Tanker Strike
data.description:
U.S. stock futures moved lower on Monday night as oil prices started rising again in late trading. Futures on the Nasdaq 100 (NDX), the Dow Jones Industrial Average (DJIA), and the S&P 500 (SPX) Index were down 0.61%, 0.26%, and 0.39%, respectively, at 8:35 a.m. EDT on March 30.

End of Quarter Sale - 50% Off TipRanks

Trade QQQ with leverage

Oil prices jumped after a report from Bloomberg said that Iran struck a Kuwaiti oil tanker in Dubai waters. Following this news, Brent crude (CM:BZ) rose 2.36% to $115.44 a barrel, while West Texas Intermediate (WTI) crude (CM:CL) gained 3.29% to $106.26 per barrel.

During Monday's regular trading session, stocks were mixed. The S&P 500 fell 0.39%, marking its third straight day of losses. The Nasdaq Composite also declined, dropping 0.73%. However, the Dow managed to edge higher, gaining 49.50 points, or 0.11%.

At the same time, there were signs of possible easing in the Middle East. President Donald Trump said the U.S. is in talks with a "more reasonable" leadership in Iran and noted that progress is being made, raising hopes that the conflict could end soon.

Investors also got some relief from Federal Reserve Chair Jerome Powell. He said inflation appears to be under control for now, and there is no immediate need to raise interest rates.

Looking ahead, traders will focus on new economic data on Tuesday. This includes the consumer confidence index for March and the JOLTS job openings report for February.
data.author: Shalu Saraf

data.title: Stock Market Today: SPY, QQQ Stumble as Trump Ramps Iran Threats; Crude Oil Jumps Past $100
data.description:
Both the S&P 500 ETF (SPY) and the Nasdaq 100 ETF (QQQ) closed Monday in the red as the U.S.-Iran war entered its fifth week.

End of Quarter Sale - 50% Off TipRanks

Trade QQQ with leverage

News That Moved the Stock Market Today

  • President Trump said the U.S. is in "serious discussions" with Iranian officials to end the war.
  • He also threatened to destroy Kharg Island and Iran's power plants, oil wells, and "possibly all desalinization plants" if Iran refuses to sign a deal and keeps the Strait of Hormuz closed.
  • Rate hike odds dropped after Fed Chair Jerome Powell said the Fed is inclined to "look through any kind of a supply shock." The odds of a 25 bps hike by year-end on the CME FedWatch tool fell to 3.8% from 22.2% on Friday.
  • Crude oil futures (CL) rose above $100 per barrel for the first time since the start of the war on reports that Iran's parliament advanced a bill to charge a toll for ships passing through the strait.
  • Aletheia Capital reiterated its "buy" rating and price target of $330 on AMD (AMD). At the same time, Erste Group lowered its rating to "hold" from "buy."
  • SpaceX is considering allocating most of its IPO shares to Morgan Stanley's (MS) E*Trade instead of Robinhood (HOOD) and SoFi (SOFI).
  • Palo Alto Networks (PANW) closed higher after its CEO, Nikesh Arora, disclosed a $10 million insider buy.

Today's Best-Performing Sector 

The financials group was the best-performing sector on Monday, driven by falling interest rate odds. With lower rates, borrowing becomes cheaper, which can increase demand for loans and support bank lending activity.

Several financial stocks led the sector's gains, including:

  • Arthur J. Gallagher & Company (AJG)
  • FactSet (FDS)
  • Aon (AON)
  • Marsh & McLennan Companies (MRSH)
  • Brown & Brown (BRO)

Today's Worst-Performing Sector 

Meanwhile, the industrials sector faced the largest drawdown amid rising oil prices. Higher energy costs increase operating costs for energy-intensive companies, which weighs on profit margins. Energy infrastructure, heavy machinery, and manufacturing firms were hit particularly hard.

Notable industrial stocks trading lower include:

  • Comfort Systems USA (FIX)
  • Generac Holdings (GNRC)
  • Emcor Group (EME)
  • GE Vernova (GEV)
  • Caterpillar (CAT)
data.author: Eddie Pan

data.title: Bill Ackman Claims a Fannie Mae & Freddie Mac Stock Surge 'Could Happen Soon'
data.description:
Federal National Mortgage Association (FNMA), commonly known as Fannie Mae, and Freddie Mac (FMCC) stocks were a hot topic on Monday following comments from Pershing Square Capital Management founder and CEO Bill Ackman. The hedge fund manager took to X to speak about high-quality businesses that are trading at extremely low prices. When commenting on the mortgage companies, he said, "And Fannie and Freddie are stupidly cheap. Asymmetry at its best. They could be a 10X and it could happen soon."

End of Quarter Sale - 50% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Interest in Fannie Mae and Freddie Mac has grown recently due to reports that the U.S. government will hold initial public offerings (IPOs) for the mortgage companies. This would see the return of FNMA and FMCC stocks to public exchanges. Currently, the shares trade over-the-counter after they were delisted from major exchanges in June 2010. This was the result of their being placed in government conservatorship due to the U.S. housing crisis.

An IPO for Fannie Mae and Freddie Mac would bring an end to government conservatorship of the companies. However, it's unclear when this would happen. Some sources have claimed the federal government is targeting IPOs for the first half of 2026. Others, such as hedge fund manager Michael Burry, said there's unlikely to be an IPO for these mortgage companies until at least 2027.

Fannie Mae and Freddie Mac Stock Movements Today

Fannie Mae stock closed out Friday down 1.62%, while Freddie Mac stock was down 4.78% when markets closed on Friday. The shares haven't performed well recently, with FNMA stock down 54.71% year-to-date and FMCC stock down 56.8% year-to-date. Fannie Mae stock has also fallen 23.1% over the past 12 months, while Freddie Mac stock has dropped 18.13% over that same period of time.
data.author: William White

data.title: FANG and CRGY: Investors Pick 2 Oil Stocks to Buy as Crude Prices Rise
data.description:
Depending on who you listen to – the media, the commentary you follow, or the latest messaging out of the Trump White House – the drama in the Middle East is either intensifying in scope or about to wind down.

End of Quarter Sale - 50% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

One thing is certain – the world's oil markets are feeling the strain. Prices have pushed higher, with WTI crude trading around $106 and Brent hovering near $112, as the conflict continues to ripple through both futures markets and the physical trade. The surge comes as it becomes clear that Iran is playing its strongest card by clamping down on the Strait of Hormuz for all its worth.

With some 20% of the world's oil traversing this narrow waterway, it's no surprise that oil markets are in flux. Not even the release of 400 million barrels of oil from strategic reserves and temporarily lifting sanctions on banned Russian and Iranian oil have succeeded in calming jittery markets.

While the world waits as the Trump administration weighs its options, some investors are looking for ways to survive the tumult by focusing on those energy stocks best positioned to capitalize on the changing dynamic.

That's where investor Lee Samaha sees opportunity. He's bullish on Diamondback Energy (NASDAQ:FANG), a Permian Basin-focused producer operating across West Texas and New Mexico, right at the core of America's most productive oil region.

In 2025, Diamondback pumped an average of 497,000 barrels of oil per day, with output climbing to 512,800 barrels per day in the fourth quarter. Just as important, it's translating that production into shareholder returns – generating $1.2 billion in adjusted free cash flow and returning 62% of that, or $734 million, through buybacks and dividends.

"A higher oil price obviously helps Diamondback," states the 5-star investor, who is among the top 4% of stock pros covered by TipRanks. Beyond the obvious pressure point of Hormuz, Samaha adds that the war has the potential to significantly set back production capabilities throughout the Gulf region.

"The pressure could be more sustained if there's greater damage to energy infrastructure in the region," adds Samaha. (To watch Lee Samaha's track record, click here)

Wall Street is feeling pretty good about FANG as well. With 21 Buys and 4 Holds, FANG enjoys a Strong Buy consensus rating. (See FANG stock forecast)
data.author: Gabe Ross

data.title: Oil Pushes Past $100 as Iran Advances Hormuz Toll Plan
data.description:
Crude oil futures (CL) climbed past $100 per barrel on Monday for the first time since the start of the U.S.-Iran war on February 28 as the Strait of Hormuz remains at a near standstill. In addition, the Majlis, Iran's parliament, is working on a bill to charge vessels passing through the passageway.

End of Quarter Sale - 50% Off TipRanks

New trading tool for QQQ bulls/bears

"Security and services for ships will be ensured through fees [for passage through the strait] stipulated by the bill," said Alaeddin Boroujerdi, a member of the Majlis' Committee for Foreign Policy and National Security.

U.S. Opposes Hormuz Toll as Passage Remains Closed

White House Press Secretary Karoline Leavitt said the U.S. opposes the toll as the war enters its fifth week. Earlier today, President Trump warned that the U.S. would destroy key Iranian energy infrastructure if the strait remains closed and if Iran refuses to sign a deal.

The passageway is responsible for roughly 20% of global oil flows, while its closure has created an oil supply crunch, sending prices higher.
data.author: Eddie Pan

data.title: Why Is AMD Stock Down Today?
data.description:
Advanced Micro Devices (AMD) stock fell on Thursday after the semiconductor company was hit with a downgrade from Erste Group analyst Stephan Lingnau. The analyst dropped AMD stock to a Hold rating from a Buy, but didn't provide a price target for the shares.

End of Quarter Sale - 50% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Lingnau gave a few reasons for the AMD stock downgrade in a research note to clients. He highlighted the fact that the company's operating margins have been lower than the sector average in recent quarters. Additionally, the analyst argued that the value of AMD is too high for its lackluster return and failure to improve operating margins.

The downgrade from Lingnau came after another blow to the stock from HSBC analyst Frank Lee on Tuesday. Lee reiterated a Buy rating for AMD stock, but lowered his price target for the shares to $185 from $200. This new price target suggests there's still a potential 18.57% upside for AMD shares.

AMD Stock Movement Today

AMD stock was down 2.55% on Thursday, but remained up 28.71% year-to-date. The shares have also increased 5.82% over the past 12 months. 2025 has been a good year for the company, with the artificial intelligence (AI) boom having increased demand for its server products. One of the company's executives recently defended AMD's server CPU prices and argued the company offers "superior value."
data.author: William White

data.title: 3 Best Stocks to Buy amid Mounting Stagflation Fears, According to Wall Street
data.description:
Global markets are grappling with the surge in oil prices triggered by the U.S.-Iran war. Given escalating tensions in the Middle East, fears of stagflation, a period of rising inflation and stagnant growth, are rising. In such a scenario, stocks in resilient sectors with stable cash flows and defensive business models can offer attractive returns. Here we will look at three such stocks that can be safer bets amid stagflation: Brookfield Infrastructure Partners (BIP), SLB (SLB), and RTX (RTX). These stocks score Wall Street's Strong Buy consensus rating and can offer attractive upside.

End of Quarter Sale - 50% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Brookfield Infrastructure Partners (NYSE:BIP) Stock

Brookfield Infrastructure operates a diversified portfolio of high-quality utilities, transport, midstream, and data assets that generate sustainable and increasing distributions for investors over the long term. 

The company is confident in its ability to capitalize on the growing demand for AI infrastructure while delivering solid cash flows and strong returns for shareholders through annual distribution growth of 5% to 9%. BIP stock offers a dividend yield of about 5%.

Given its resilient business model, solid cash flows, and attractive yield, BIP can be considered a suitable investment during stagflation. Wall Street has a Strong Buy consensus rating on Brookfield Infrastructure stock based on five Buys and one Hold. The average BIP stock price target of $45 implies 28% upside potential. BIP stock has risen more than 18% over the past year.
data.author: Sirisha Bhogaraju

data.title: CrowdStrike Stock (CRWD) Gains on Analyst Support as Anthropic Poses "Machine-Speed Cyberwar" Threat
data.description:
CrowdStrike (CRWD) shares rose about 3% early Monday as more analysts continued to back the cybersecurity firm amid renewed fears of artificial intelligence disruption to the industry.

End of Quarter Sale - 50% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Anthropic's Upcoming Model Sparks New AI Fears

Wolfe Research's five-star analyst Alex Zukin backed the company despite noting that AI startup Anthropic's upcoming model "has the potential to ignite a machine-speed cyberwar the likes of which we have never seen." Zukin elevated CRWD from Peer Perform (Hold) to Outperform (Buy) and set a price target of $450, implying about 22% upside.

The analyst's comment comes days after Anthropic's new model tailored for cybersecurity leaked to the media, fueling a new round of AI jitters that sank cybersecurity stocks such as Palo Alto Networks (PANW) and Zscaler (ZS) on Friday.

According to media reports, Anthropic is working on a new system called the Claude Mythos that could be its most powerful model yet. CrowdStrike's shares also shuddered last week following reports that e-commerce giant Amazon (AMZN) is also working on an in-house AI agent to oversee sales and business development tasks. CrowdStrike serves as a cybersecurity partner to Amazon's Business Prime customers.

Wolfe Flags Revenue Acceleration for CrowdStrike

However, Zukin only sees upside for CrowdStrike from the development. He believes it could force customers to focus on a smaller group of cybersecurity software companies, driving more vendor consolidation.

The Wolfe analyst also contended that this could help CrowdStrike rapidly expand its annual recurring revenue in fiscal 2027. Zukin's comment emphasizes a sentiment that has also been shared by several other analysts on Wall Street.

For instance, BTIG last week dismissed media framing of the new Anthropic model as risking a new "arms race" as "completely wrong." The financial services firm argued that the risk of AI heightens cyber risks that would drive more demand to vendors.

Is CrowdStrike a Buy or Sell?

Across Wall Street, CrowdStrike's shares remain a Strong Buy based on analysts' consensus rating. This breaks down to 28 Buys and nine Holds issued over the past three months.

In addition, the average CRWD price target of $483.32 implies about 27% upside from current trading levels.
data.author: Solomon Oladipupo

data.title: Iren Stock Caught in Big Tech's AI Crossfire — Is a Relief Rally in Sight?
data.description:
Iren Limited's (IREN) shares fell over 5% on Monday morning, extending losses seen in the prior two trading sessions. The timing of a new rally may be tied to improving sentiment toward Big Tech or to a new milestone for the Australian AI cloud company.

End of Quarter Sale - 50% Off TipRanks

Trade CRWV with leverage

Why Are Iren Shares under Pressure?

Iren is traded as a proxy for the AI infrastructure buildout, and its wobbling performance comes as shares in several Big Tech companies have dropped over the past month due to worries about their massive AI capital expenditure and timing of payoff.

Since the start of the year, the Sydney-based company's shares have declined over 12% despite their massive rally in 2025. Shares in its neo-cloud peers, New Jersey-based CoreWeave (CRWV) and Amsterdam-based Nebius (NBIS), also slumped during the first hours of trading on Monday, worsening losses logged over the previous two sessions.

This underscores how pressure on Big Tech is rippling across the wider industry, including these companies. They have emerged as key partners to tech supermajors, supplying additional cloud computing capacity to support data center and AI infrastructure buildouts. 

What's Next for Iren Stock?

Beyond investor anxiety around Big Tech, Iren's plan to raise up to $6 billion through an expansion of its equity program also recently fueled a sell-off on share dilution fears. This is despite plans to issue the new shares in small, continuous batches at current market prices.

Nonetheless, several analysts remain bullish on Iren, even as the company has entered into a new purchase agreement with Nvidia (NVDA) to expand its graphics processing unit fleet to 150,000. Iren also plans to spend about $3.5 billion during the second half of this year on its infrastructure — a move H.C. Wainwright's five-star analyst Mike Colonnese believes shows that "customer demand is strong enough."

Is IREN Stock a Strong Buy?

Across Wall Street, Iren's shares are currently considered a Moderate Buy based on analysts' consensus rating. This breaks down to eight Buys, two Holds, and one Sell assigned by 11 analysts over the past three months.

However, the average IREN price target of $79.78 implies about 137% upside.
data.author: Solomon Oladipupo

data.title: 'It's Time to Buy,' Says Morgan Stanley on Meta Stock (META)
data.description:
Meta Platforms (META) may be facing near-term pressure, but Morgan Stanley believes the stock is now an attractive buying opportunity. In a recent note, top analyst Brian Nowak lowered his price target to $775 from $825 but kept an Overweight rating. His revised price target of $775 still implies a 47% gain from current levels.

End of Quarter Sale - 50% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Why Morgan Stanley Is Bullish on Meta Despite Near-Term Risks

The analyst said investor sentiment around Meta has weakened in recent months, with the stock down about 20% so far this year due to concerns over its huge infrastructure spending plan. Investors are also unsure about returns from its AI investments and its long-term strategy. More recently, worries about the ad market and possible regulatory risks have added to the pressure.

However, Nowak believes these concerns are already reflected in the stock price. He noted that Meta is now trading at around 15 times its estimated 2027 earnings, which is below its long-term average. In his view, this creates a favorable entry point for investors.

Importantly, the analyst said his earnings estimates already factor in some softness in the ad market. He has reduced ad revenue estimates for 2026 by about 1% to stay conservative. Even with that, the current valuation still looks appealing.

At the same time, he pointed to potential cost savings as another upside factor. Reports suggest Meta could reduce its workforce by about 20%, which may lead to significant savings. If that happens, it could add meaningfully to future earnings or help offset any weakness in advertising.

What Is the Price Target for Meta?

Turning to Wall Street, analysts have a Strong Buy consensus rating on META stock based on 40 Buys, five Holds, and zero Sells assigned in the past three months, as indicated by the graphic below. Furthermore, the average META price target of $865.58 per share implies 64.65% upside potential.
data.author: Shalu Saraf
